headline: Italy opens first DMA probe into Apple over iCloud access
summary: Italy’s regulator examines whether Apple gives rival cloud providers equal access to iOS and iPadOS system features.

content.title: Italy opens first DMA probe into Apple over iCloud access
content.summary: Italy’s regulator examines whether Apple gives rival cloud providers equal access to iOS and iPadOS system features.

thumbnail.caption: Apple is required to ensure that third-party providers of consumer cloud services can interact freely and effectively with the company’s operating systems.

headline: India Bans Pavel Durov Telegram: GRAM and Crypto Hammered, Whatsapp Accused
summary: GRAM dropped between 2% after India ordered Google and Apple to delist Telegram from their app stores, cutting off access for an estimated 104 to 150 million users, and Pavel Durov is not happy. It’s cutting Telegram’s single largest national user base. Executed under Section 69A of India’s Information Technology ...

headline: Intel Stock Pops as Chip-Making Bet Moves Closer to Paying Off
summary: Intel  stock was climbing early Wednesday.  A chip-sector rally was being reinforced by progress on manufacturing.  The company said late Tuesday that it has entered “risk production” for the 18A-P variant of its 18A manufacturing process.

content.title: Intel Stock Pops as Chip-Making Bet Moves Closer to Paying Off
content.summary: Intel  stock was climbing early Wednesday.  A chip-sector rally was being reinforced by progress on manufacturing.  The company said late Tuesday that it has entered “risk production” for the 18A-P variant of its 18A manufacturing process.

headline: How America Got to SpaceX: The Long Trail of Big IPOs That Began in 1783
summary: SpaceX’s  $75 billion initial public offering shattered market records, turned founder Elon Musk into the world’s first trillionaire, and opened fresh debates over corporate valuations and governance.  It’s not the first time an IPO has shaken things up.  IPOs have been reshaping markets since the Bank of North America’s 1783 debut got it all started for the young republic.

content.title: How America Got to SpaceX: The Long Trail of Big IPOs That Began in 1783
content.summary: SpaceX’s  $75 billion initial public offering shattered market records, turned founder Elon Musk into the world’s first trillionaire, and opened fresh debates over corporate valuations and governance.  It’s not the first time an IPO has shaken things up.  IPOs have been reshaping markets since the Bank of North America’s 1783 debut got it all started for the young republic.

headline: Apple (AAPL) Stock After 129% Five-Year Rally Is There Still Value Here
summary: If you are wondering whether Apple stock still offers value after its strong run, the answer depends heavily on how you look at price versus fundamentals. At a last close of US$299.24, Apple has returned 3.0% over the past week, was roughly flat over the past month with a 0.3% decline, and has delivered 10.4% year to date, 53.6% over 1 year, 64.0% over 3 years, and 129.1% over 5 years. Recent coverage around Apple has continued to focus on its role as a large US tech stock and a key holding...

content.title: Apple (AAPL) Stock After 129% Five-Year Rally Is There Still Value Here

headline: Global Hedge Funds Doubled Down on Technology and Semiconductor Names in May Despite Persistent Macro Risks, According to Hazeltree
summary: NEW YORK & LONDON, June 17, 2026--​Global hedge funds maintained a strong appetite for technology and semiconductor companies in May, continuing a trend that has persisted throughout much of 2026 despite inflation concerns, rising energy prices, ongoing conflict in the Middle East, and questions surrounding fiscal sustainability.
